description: Hand-labeled prompts for evaluating auto_context.py precision/recall across Python, TypeScript, and Rust fixtures. Each prompt maps to the top files a competent engineer would open first to work on that task. expected_files is ordered by relevance.

python.prompt: add rate limiting to the login endpoint so users can't brute force passwords
python.notes: rate_limit_decorator already wraps handle_login in router.py; user wants tighter limit on /login

python.prompt: where is hash_password defined and which files call it
python.notes: hash_password lives in crypto.py; login.py uses verify_password from same module

python.prompt: make the session TTL configurable per environment via the Settings class
python.notes: Settings.session_ttl lives in settings.py; create_session reads it

python.prompt: refactor find_user_by_email to use async database queries
python.notes: find_user_by_email lives in queries.py, called from login.py, uses get_db_connection

python.prompt: auth_middleware should log the user_id not just warn on unauth
add a created_at column to the users table via a new migration
python.notes: MIGRATIONS list in migrations.py; User model in models.py

python.prompt: register a /logout endpoint on APIRouter that invalidates the session
python.notes: APIRouter.add in router.py; SessionManager.invalidate in session.py

python.prompt: send a welcome email via EmailClient when a new user signs up
python.notes: send_email + EmailClient in email.py; signup-like flow lives near login.py

python.prompt: convert get_db_connection to use a connection pool and close idle conns
python.notes: get_db_connection + close_connection in database.py; callers in queries.py + migrations.py

python.prompt: expose RATE_LIMIT_CONFIG through the Settings object instead of a module constant
python.notes: RATE_LIMIT_CONFIG lives in rate_limit.py; Settings class in settings.py

python.prompt: verify_password returns True for empty string on malformed stored hash
python.notes: verify_password is in crypto.py; only caller is login.py validate_credentials

python.prompt: load_config should also read from process environment if .env missing

typescript.prompt: add rate limiting to the login handler to block brute force
typescript.notes: rateLimit decorator in rateLimit.ts; handleLogin in login.ts; router wires both

typescript.prompt: where is hashPassword defined and which files call it
typescript.notes: hashPassword lives in crypto.ts; login.ts is the caller

typescript.prompt: make session TTL configurable via loadConfig so it varies per environment
typescript.notes: Settings interface + loadConfig in settings.ts; SessionManager reads it

typescript.prompt: refactor findUserByEmail to return a Promise and batch connection reuse
typescript.notes: findUserByEmail in users.ts, called from login.ts, needs getDbConnection

typescript.prompt: authMiddleware should log unauthorized user ids via the logger util
add a created_at column to users via a new migration
typescript.notes: MIGRATIONS array in migrations.ts; User interface in users.ts

typescript.prompt: register a /logout route on the router that invalidates the session
typescript.notes: createRouter in router.ts; SessionManager.invalidate in session.ts

typescript.prompt: send a welcome email via EmailClient when a new user signs up
typescript.notes: sendEmail + EmailClient in email.ts; signup-like flow lives in login.ts

typescript.prompt: convert getDbConnection to a pooled connection with closeConnection called on idle
typescript.notes: getDbConnection + closeConnection in database.ts; callers in users.ts + migrations.ts

typescript.prompt: the createApp bootstrap should load config before wiring the router
typescript.notes: createApp in app.ts wires router + config

rust.prompt: add a tighter rate limit on the login endpoint to block brute force
rust.notes: RateLimiter in rate_limit.rs; handle_login in login.rs; Router wires both

rust.prompt: where is hash_password defined in the crate and who calls it
rust.notes: hash_password lives in crypto.rs; login.rs is the caller

rust.prompt: make the session TTL configurable per environment via the Settings struct
rust.notes: Settings struct in settings.rs; session.rs reads it

rust.prompt: refactor find_user_by_email to async and use the pooled connection
rust.notes: find_user_by_email in queries.rs, called by login.rs, uses get_db_connection

rust.prompt: auth_middleware should log the user id via log_warn when unauth
rust.notes: middleware.rs uses logging fns and SessionManager

rust.prompt: add a created_at column to users via a new migration entry
rust.notes: MIGRATIONS const in migrations.rs; User struct in models.rs

rust.prompt: register a /logout route on Router that invalidates the session
rust.notes: Router in router.rs; SessionManager::invalidate in session.rs

rust.prompt: send a welcome email via EmailClient when a new user signs up
rust.notes: send_email + EmailClient in email.rs; signup-like flow lives in login.rs

rust.prompt: convert get_db_connection to a pooled Connection and drop idle conns
rust.notes: Connection + get_db_connection in database.rs; callers in queries.rs + migrations.rs

rust.prompt: load_config should also read from std::env when the dotenv file is missing
